header.location: Portland, OR (Open to Remote or Relocation)

summary: I'm Jamie—a curious, people-loving (97% extrovert!) People & Organizational Development professional who thrives on solving problems through data, building evidence-based programs, and partnering with stakeholders for lasting impact. Expect me to keep elevating people experience through human-centered, data-driven approaches.

bullets.text: With a consulting team, audit 300+ {data|leave cases} (${data|362K}) to refute client cost assumptions and inform policy
bullets.reason: ODN Project 1 (leave-cost diagnosis) — collective 'we' voice; real metric; data/insight skill the JD values for connecting work to business priorities

bullets.text: Run {cross|focus groups and interviews} with an NGO leadership team to diagnose decision-making and accountability gaps
bullets.reason: ODN Project 2 (NGO leadership) — separate project, never blended; stakeholder/diagnostic skill, kept light (part-time role)

bullets.text: Manage {prog|20+ programs} and 10+ vendors, refining {coord|program operations} and {cross|communications strategy} via feedback analysis
bullets.original: Manage 20+ programs and 10+ vendors, refining program positioning, delivery strategy, and operations via feedback analysis
bullets.reason: Lead with program ops scale + communications strategy — real program-management work the JD's stakeholder/coordination duties value

bullets.text: Collaborate with {cross|cross-functional teams} and 10+ vendors to {prog|launch 20+ programs}; conduct {ex|engagement} research for HR
bullets.original: Conduct needs assessments with 25+ global stakeholders to identify learning gaps and design 3 new educational programs
bullets.reason: Surfaces cross-functional collaboration + employee engagement research — maps to JD's 'partner with Talent, HRBPs, and Communications' and community-building programs

bullets.text: Build {coord|cross-functional processes} and documentation for 70+ staff, streamlining operations and cutting onboarding time by 75%
bullets.original: Develop guides and workflows for 70+ cross-functional staff, improving program knowledge and reducing onboarding time by 75%
bullets.reason: Process + documentation for program ops — maps to JD's 'strong execution skills' and program logistics support

bullets.text: Facilitate {prog|listening sessions} with 25+ {cross|stakeholders}, launching 3 {prog|programs} aligned with stakeholder needs (78% {prog|enrollment rate})
bullets.original: Facilitate training webinars for 600+ participants, enhancing program understanding and driving a 78% client conversion rate
bullets.reason: Listening sessions with stakeholders + 3 real programs (78% enrollment) — genuine program-design evidence; 'enrollment rate' (not 'conversion') per people-impact framing

bullets.text: Led a manager effectiveness initiative, transforming {data|feedback data} into {data|actionable reports} that informed {ex|engagement} strategies
Analyzed 2,000+ {data|employee survey responses} to amplify employee voice and guide C-suite decisions on {ex|engagement} drivers
bullets.original: Analyzed 2,000+ employee experiences to amplify employee voice and guide C-suite decisions on satisfaction drivers
bullets.reason: 'Survey responses' surfaces the listening/data collection work — relevant to JD's emphasis on understanding employee sentiment and culture

bullets.text: Consulted on strategic plans and delivered {prog|internal training} on DEI, Inclusive Leadership, and performance review
Elevated {ex|employee experience} via 5 {cross|cross-functional} projects focusing on {prog|engagement}, rewards & recognition, and L&D
Initiated a DEI pilot with an {prog|Inclusive Leadership workshop} for 12 senior leaders, expanded to 23 global locations
bullets.reason: Lead bullet — real Inclusive Leadership workshop pilot (scaled to 23 locations) is Jamie's strongest true inclusion-program evidence

bullets.text: Strengthened {ex|employee connection} via an {prog|HR newsletter} and award-winning well-being programs (1,000+ competitors)
bullets.reason: Moved up — newsletter + well-being programs maps to JD's culture integration, community-building events, and employee connection programs

bullets.text: Facilitated organizational change by directing a Mergers & Acquisitions project, coordinating {cross|cross-functional stakeholders}
Optimized {coord|onboarding processes} across 3 global locations by conducting process analysis and clarifying job responsibilities
Developed proficiency across various HR functions, ranging from recruitment and compensation to performance management
Filled an international role by drafting job descriptions, posting openings, and researching compensation across markets
Engaged global talent via {cross|personalized communication} and customer service, increasing applications by 566% in 2 months
Organized {coord|recruiting events} and moderated C-suite panels at top 3 universities, reaching 230+ students and 80+ applicants

bullets: Relevant Coursework: Organizational Psychology, Research Methods, Group Dynamics & Leadership, Consumer Psychology

jd.meta: Cambia Health Solutions — Community and Culture Program Manager · Portland, OR (hybrid 3d/wk) · $84,200–$113,900 + 10% bonus · HR Team · Req R-6627 · Posted ~Jun 8, 2026 · ⚠️ H1B FAIL — zero HR/People/culture filings; proceeding as explicit override (transfer case, active immigration counsel)

sections.title: Role Overview
sections.content: Hands-on role at the intersection of {ex|culture, inclusion, employee connection, and community}. Translates Cambia's commitment to inclusion and connection into {prog|employee programs, experiences, and practices}. Works across ERG management, culture integration, event planning, community impact, and {cross|cross-functional collaboration with Talent, HRBPs, Comms}.

items.text: Primary HR point of contact and {prog|program manager for Employee Resource Groups}
Support {prog|ERG leaders} with programming, logistics, development resources, and visibility
Help grow and sustain {prog|employee clubs and communities} across the organization
Lead {prog|program management for culture integration initiatives} connecting employees across teams, locations, backgrounds
Support return-to-office efforts via meaningful in-office experiences that foster {ex|connection}
Drive expansion of Blue Squad and {prog|community-building programs} to all office locations
Manage planning and execution of {coord|employee engagement events}
Partner with {cross|Talent, HRBPs, and Communications} to integrate culture and inclusion into key talent practices
Serve as resource and {cross|thought partner to leaders} strengthening team culture
3–5 years in employee engagement, culture, inclusion, talent, or related HR/people functions ✅ (~3 years OD/PM/HR — meets floor)
Experience managing programs or events with multiple stakeholders ✅ (InGenius 20+ programs, Kronos events, Vestas DEI pilot)
Familiarity with ERGs, employee communities, or volunteer/foundation partnerships ⚠️ (ODN Oregon is a professional volunteer network — adjacent; no direct ERG management experience)
Strong organizational and project management skills ✅

sections.title: H1B Status
sections.content: ⚠️ FAIL — h1bdata.info: Cambia Health Solutions = 91 LCA filings, ALL tech-side (SWE, data/AI, tech PM). ZERO HR/People/culture/comms. Regence entities: 2 filings, pediatricians only. Not cap-exempt. Proceeding as explicit override: Jamie is a transfer case (not lottery), Cambia has active immigration counsel, and she is drawn to the Portland hybrid role. Long-shot H1B expectations set.

changeLog.change: Summary kept as the canonical broad 3-pillar People & Organizational Development umbrella (data analysis / evidence-based programs / stakeholder collaboration) per JAMIE_FEEDBACK_RULES §0+§1 — NOT niched into culture/belonging language the experience doesn't support.

Selected Pair B over Pair A for this role. Pair A (Upskill/Empower + $362K) emphasizes data analytics and quantified HR cost findings — less relevant than the diagnostic listening and redesign work for a culture/ERG program role. Pair B surfaces stakeholder interviews + leadership partnership to redesign engagement structures, which maps to JD's 'thought partner to leaders' and culture integration work.

changeLog.section: InGenius — Set B (Engagement) with community tilt
changeLog.change: Used Set B (engagement framing). Bullet 1: program ops + communications strategy (ERG program manager needs). Bullet 2: cross-functional collaboration + employee engagement research (culture integration + HRBP partnership). Bullet 3: cross-functional processes + documentation (execution skills). Bullet 4: listening sessions + community needs + 78% program enrollment rate. 'Program enrollment rate' (not 'client conversion rate') — engagement/HR framing.

changeLog.section: Vestas — DEI lead, newsletter moved up
changeLog.change: Reordered: DEI/Inclusive Leadership workshop to lead (direct ERG/belonging bridge — workshop for leaders expanded to 23 global locations mirrors ERG program support + culture integration). Newsletter + well-being moved to #2 (community-building + engagement programs). M&A and onboarding stay but are deprioritized.
Changed 'employee experiences' to 'employee survey responses' — surfaces the listening/data collection work relevant to JD's culture assessment and employee voice emphasis.
Kept standard Kronos bullets. Bullet 4 (recruiting events + C-suite panels) gets keyword markup to surface event coordination credential.

Project Management (Asana, Airtable) + HRIS (SAP, Rippling, ADP) + SharePoint/Notion (knowledge mgmt for program resources). Event Planning added (direct JD requirement). LMS dropped (not relevant for this role). HRCI cert kept (HR role). GenAI Tools included as standard.
Per standing rule: included exact canonical courses — Educational Psychology, Practicum in Education Studies, Psychological Testing, Learning and Motivation.
